theme: Give every surface a pulse — the office should feel like it breathes even when nobody's watching

ideas.title: Activity Log: Filter by Worker
ideas.description: The activity log filters by event type but has NO per-worker filter. Add a second row of chips — one per roster member (emoji + first name). Clicking sets a worker-name filter; entries get double-filtered (type AND worker). Clicking again clears. Roster array already available in App.jsx — ~15 lines logic, ~3 lines JSX. WHY: 'What did [person] do today?' is the #1 question. Right now requires manual scrolling. This makes it one click.

ideas.title: Conference Table in the Lower Floor
ideas.description: Bottom 30-35% of office floor is dead space — only the door sits there. Add a round conference table at ~left:40%, top:70% as a CSS circle (~13% diameter) with SVG chair arcs. Vibe-reactive: 'crushing' = all chairs occupied with role-color dots; 'in-flow' = 3-4 chairs; 'slow-day' = one chair + coffee cup; 'on-fire' = red indicator, scattered chairs; 'after-hours' = lamp off, chair sideways. WHY: Biggest visual upgrade available. Makes the dead lower third tell a story without new data.

ideas.title: Ambient Office Sounds Toggle
ideas.description: Add a tiny speaker icon in the Office tab header. Clicking it plays subtle ambient sounds: keyboard clacking when workers are active, coffee machine gurgle during idle, rain on windows during after-hours. Use Web Audio API with 3-4 small MP3s (<50KB each). Volume slider on hover. State in localStorage. WHY: Sound is the fastest way to make a flat page feel like a place. People leave ambient tabs open all day — give them a reason to keep this one.

ideas.title: Bloberto's Monitor Shows Live Stats
ideas.description: The manager desk monitor is a dark rectangle with a glow — inert despite being front-center. Add SVG content inside .mgr-desk__monitor: 3-bar mini chart (working/idle/error counts), blinking 2-letter vibe abbreviation, tiny pulse line that twitches when errors > 0. All as child SVG elements sized to existing element. WHY: Bloberto's desk is the visual anchor. Making his monitor live makes him feel like he's actually managing.

ideas.title: Shared CharacterAvatar Component Across Tabs
ideas.description: Dashboard worker cards use role emoji avatars while Office tab uses custom SVG CharacterAvatars. They look like different products. Extract CharacterAvatar to shared component, import in App.jsx, replace .worker-avatar div (getRoleEmoji()) with CharacterAvatar at 40px. All props already on worker object. WHY: Visual consistency = cohesive product. Luna's moon, Carlos's hat, Maya's hair should appear everywhere.

ideas.title: Desk Items That Accumulate Through the Day
ideas.description: Each worker's desk starts clean. As they complete tasks, small SVG items appear: coffee cups, sticky notes, crumpled paper. By end of a busy day the desk is cluttered. After-hours vibe triggers a 'cleaning' animation that resets them. Track item count from activity entries filtered by worker. WHY: Visual storytelling of work accumulation without any text. Shows effort at a glance.

ideas.title: Mini Notification Toast on New Activity
ideas.description: When a new activity entry appears (polled data changes), show a small toast notification at bottom-right of the Office tab: '[emoji] [name] [action]' that slides in, stays 4s, slides out. CSS-only animation triggered by key change. WHY: The office floor currently gives no feedback when something happens. Toasts create the feeling of a living system without requiring the user to check the dashboard tab.

ideas.title: Bloberto Strategy Deck Easter Egg
ideas.description: Double-click Bloberto to open a satirical full-screen 'Q1 Velocity Review' modal. 3 slides: (1) Team Velocity Overview with real numbers on fake bar charts labeled 'Synergy/Alignment/Vibes', (2) Star Player Spotlight with highest-progress worker and absurd titles ('Chief Velocity Officer'), (3) Strategic Insights with random corporate quotes. Arrow keys navigate, Escape closes. WHY: Bloberto has the richest personality but no interaction path. This is the feature people share links to.
